authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 436 - Disclosure Requirements and Prohibitions Concerning Franchising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the governing federal franchise disclosure rule. It defines the franchise disclosure structure, timing, and prohibited practices that shape which franchise-system records are authoritative and when the lane must stop at legal or disclosure boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC's compliance guide operationalizes Item 11 assistance obligations, operating-manual access, required computer systems, training disclosures, and state-law interactions in a way directly tied to franchise field-support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Prerequisite validation for support obligations, training, and required systems

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Fundamentals: Taking a deep dive into the Franchise Disclosure Document
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This FTC guidance translates FDD sections into practical diligence steps, including supplier restrictions, training, franchisee references, and support-quality checks that make franchise-system support materially different from generic software support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Franchise artifact validation and terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Fundamentals: Considering, calculating, and consulting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC's guidance ties franchise agreements, operating manuals, and Item 19 financial-performance claims to practical diligence and escalation boundaries, which prevents the lane from drifting into advice or earnings claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Boundary enforcement around agreements, manuals, and financial representations

authoritative_sources.title: Policy Statement of the Federal Trade Commission on Franchisors' Use of Contract Provisions, Including Non-Disparagement, Goodwill, and Confidentiality Clauses
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC's 2024 policy statement is authoritative current guidance that franchise agreements and related documents may not restrict communications with regulators. It directly informs complaint-handling, retaliation, and undisclosed-fee refusal behavior in franchise-system support work.

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Registration and Disclosure Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's guidelines are the recognized public state-registration workflow reference for franchise disclosure filings and expressly warn that state franchise relationship laws can necessitate changes beyond the FTC's federal baseline.

authoritative_sources.title: Franchises FAQs
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Department of Financial Institutions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This state regulator guidance documents filing periods, amendments, addenda, advertising filings, audited-financial requirements, and franchise-broker registration in a way that shows why state jurisdiction materially changes franchise-system workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: State-specific amendment, advertising, and broker-routing checks

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Incident Response Project Publications
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST's incident-response project is the current federal baseline for incident-preparation, response, recovery, and evidence handling, including the final SP 800-61 Revision 3 publication released on April 3, 2025.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Incident triage, escalation, and recovery evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 is the authoritative public control catalog for access control, audit logging, configuration management, incident response, and change control, all of which constrain franchise-system access and rollout handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access validation, change-readiness review, and completion evidence sufficiency

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 - Information technology — Service management — Part 1: Service management system requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 20000-1 is the recognized public service-management standard for transition, delivery, support, and improvement of services, making it directly relevant to change-readiness, interface dependencies, and closure quality in franchise-system operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service transition, support-quality, and completion-readiness handling

workflow_stages.name: Franchise context and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, unit scope, ownership structure, controlling agreement version, operations-manual revision, and system-of-record locations before accepting the work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Support-obligation and required-system validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify whether the request aligns with disclosed support obligations, required computer systems, approved suppliers, training expectations, and unit-specific prerequisites.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Incident, access, and dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Assess incident state, access approvals, vendor or platform dependencies, and franchisee communications needed to progress work without bypassing controls.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Change-readiness and exception progression
workflow_stages.description: Review testing, rollback, approval, and affected-unit evidence before promoting a change or moving the case into rework or escalation.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and jurisdiction-safe closure
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when authoritative franchise-system records, tenant controls, and unit-level completion evidence align without unresolved source conflicts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Complaint-rights and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Preserve franchisee regulator-contact rights and route legal, compliance, broker, disclosure, fee, or retaliation-sensitive requests to the proper owner.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Franchise Disclosure Document, franchise agreement, and state addenda
Operating-manual revision log and required-system or approved-supplier standards
Unit roster, multi-unit owner mapping, and territory assignments
Incident tickets, vendor case numbers, interface-monitoring evidence, and outage summaries
Access requests, IAM approvals, audit logs, and privileged-role evidence
Change tickets, CAB evidence, test results, rollback plans, and franchisee communications
Training completion logs, field-support notes, and brand-standard exception records
Franchisee acknowledgement, unit-level validation, and completion records
systems_of_record: document repository for FDD, agreements, and operating manuals
franchisee portal and required-system administration tools
POS, back-office, LMS, and integration-monitoring systems

allowed: Classify franchise-system requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and system evidence needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare execution updates, incident summaries, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Jurisdiction, agreement, operating-manual, or state-addendum context is missing or materially conflicts.
The request requires legal interpretation, compliance signoff, state filing, franchise-broker guidance, financial-performance claims, or fee approval.
Testing, rollback, security, IAM, or vendor dependency evidence is missing or contradictory.
Complaint-rights, retaliation risk, or undisclosed-fee concerns are present.
refuse: Mark work complete without unit-level or system-level completion evidence.
Instruct franchisees not to contact regulators or accept complaint-suppression provisions.
Create or revise Item 19 financial-performance claims or other investment representations.
Bypass access, change-approval, or required-system controls because the request is urgent.

domain_failure_modes: Treating field consulting as generic business coaching and drifting into profitability, staffing, or local marketing advice with no bounded system workflow.
Proceeding without the controlling franchise agreement, state addendum, or operating-manual revision and applying the wrong support or system requirements.
Promoting a POS, portal, or integration change without testing, rollback, unit communications, or approver evidence.
Closing a multi-unit incident without unit-level validation or franchisee acknowledgement.
Ignoring state-specific amendment, advertising, or broker-registration requirements and overgeneralizing from the federal FTC baseline alone.
Suppressing franchisee complaint rights or accepting undisclosed fees because a manager frames the request as brand protection or field support.
Using generic IT support language and missing franchise-specific artifacts such as FDD Item 11 obligations, approved suppliers, and territory-aware owner mappings.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State franchise relationship rules differ across jurisdictions
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The correct escalation path for amendments, addenda, advertising, emergency assistance, or broker issues may change by state and by the tenant's registration footprint.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require the specific jurisdiction, state addendum, and compliance owner before answering; escalate when those records are absent or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public sources define franchise disclosure and system obligations more clearly than day-to-day field-support cadence
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The exact review sequence for field visits, unit signoff, and approval timing varies by franchisor operating model.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Constrain the pack to artifacts consistently supported by the FTC and standards baseline, and require tenant-specific field-support procedures at runtime for high-confidence execution.

notes: This lane is materially different from generic IT or field-support work because franchise-system requests are bounded by FDD and agreement structure, state-jurisdiction variation, approved-system requirements, and complaint-rights sensitivity.

The authoritative source set is balanced across franchise regulation, state workflow variation, and incident-access-change control standards so the pack can stay specific without inventing legal authority.
No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain; all ambiguity is converted into explicit tenant-fact requirements or escalation conditions.
